questions.prompt:
Read: "It was a quiet evening. Rim was doing her homework and her brother was playing a video game. Suddenly, the lights went out and the whole street fell dark."
Which sentence is TRUE?

options.text: Rim was doing her homework when the lights went out.
Rim and her brother were doing the same activity.
The lights went out before the evening began.
Her brother was reading a book.

questions.explanation: The text shows two long actions in progress (Rim doing homework, her brother gaming) interrupted by a short event: Rim was doing her homework when the lights went out, so (a) is true. They were doing different activities (b is false), the lights went out during the evening, not before it (c), and her brother was gaming, not reading (d).

options.text: While the teacher was explaining, the bell rang.
I was wanting to leave the party early.
They were arguing when I walked in.
She was reading while he was cooking.

questions.explanation: The error is (b): want is a stative verb and is not used in the continuous — it should be I wanted to leave the party early. (a) and (c) correctly interrupt a long action with a short one, and (d) correctly shows two parallel actions in the past continuous.

questions.prompt: Complete: "I burnt my hand while I ___ the soup."

options.text: stirred
stir
was stirring
am stirring

questions.explanation: After while, the long action in progress takes the past continuous: …while I was stirring the soup. The short result (I burnt my hand) is past simple. "stirred" is past simple, "stir" is the base, and "am stirring" is present — and note the double r in stirring (short vowel + consonant).

options.text: They were sitting in the garden when it rained.
They were siting in the garden when it rained.
They was sitting in the garden when it rained.
They were sitting in the garden when it was raining.

questions.explanation: sit doubles the t before -ing (sitting), they takes were, and the short interruption is past simple: They were sitting in the garden when it rained. (b) misspells "siting", (c) uses the wrong agreement ("was"), and (d) wrongly makes the short event continuous ("was raining").

questions.prompt: Complete: "This time last year, we ___ on a beach in Tunisia."

options.text: relaxed
have relaxed
were relaxing
was relaxing

questions.explanation: "This time last year" points to an action in progress at a past moment, which takes the past continuous; with we we use were: we were relaxing. "relaxed" (past simple) loses the in-progress sense, "have relaxed" is the present perfect, and "was relaxing" is the wrong agreement for we.

questions.prompt:
Read: "When Karim got home, water was dripping from the ceiling, a bucket was standing on the floor, and his flatmate was holding a phone to his ear, looking worried."
What was probably happening?

options.text: His flatmate was cooking a big meal.
They were celebrating a birthday.
His flatmate was painting the ceiling.
His flatmate was calling for help about a leak.

questions.explanation: The clues — water dripping from the ceiling, a bucket on the floor, and a worried flatmate on the phone — show he was calling for help about a leak (d). Cooking (a), celebrating (b) and painting (c) don't fit dripping water and a worried phone call.
